source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Crane Other Lifting Device

event.top_event: Severed fingertip during compressor head relocation
actions_taken: First aid administered to injured employee
Employee evacuated via helicopter to medical facility
event.summary: On September 11, 2017 at approximately 1138 hours, an employee severed the tip of his left index finger while attempting to relocate a compressor head without using the overhead hoist. The employee was tilting a compressor head using a 1-inch pipe when the weight caused him to lose balance. As the compressor head fell, the employee's finger was caught between the pipe and another compressor head on the floor. The employee was transported to a medical facility for treatment.
key_phrases: not using the proper tools for the task
did not follow the task steps and hazard controls as documented on the JSA

hazards.name: Pinch point between pipe and compressor head

threats.name: Failure to use overhead hoist for equipment relocation
Improper tool selection (1-inch pipe instead of hoist)
Loss of grip/balance during manual handling

consequences.name: Employee evacuation and medical treatment required

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and prescribe safe work procedures

supporting_text: Prior to the work being performed, a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was completed which included the task steps, potential hazards and the hazard controls that were specific to the task being performed.
If the IE would have followed the task steps and hazard controls as documented on the JSA by utilizing the overhead chain hoist to relocate the compressor heads the incident would have been avoided.

The probable cause of the accident is not using the proper tools for the task. The IE did not follow the task steps and hazard controls as documented on the JSA by utilizing the overhead chain hoist to relocate the compressor heads.

controls.name: Overhead chain hoist
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to safely relocate heavy compressor heads

supporting_text: The compressor heads were removed utilizing the overhead hoist and placed on the floor of the compressor building.
In an attempt to make space, the IE tilted a compressor head on its side using a piece of 1" pipe connected to the top of the compressor head and walked it to a different location.

As per the Task Steps located on the Job Safety Analysis form, the injured employee should have utilized the overhead chain hoist to relocate the compressor heads which could have alleviated the possibility of coming in contact with sharp edges or a pinch point.

controls.name: Impact resistant gloves
controls.barrier_role: Personal protective equipment to reduce severity of hand injuries

supporting_text: As the IE removed his impact resistant gloves, he discovered the tip of his left index finger had been severed.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
